repo_prior_art.cite: Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis: finite FWL/loading algebra for staggered switch-on bases; eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1: prior BJS/SA/CS/W IF-loading proposal

published_prior_art.cite: chen_santanna_xie_2025 + borusyak_jaravel_spiess_2024_restud: EIF and efficient/imputation IF anchor; standard asymptotic-linear representation is not the new kernel

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1: quotient row-space IF frontier using Gamma_m(pi,Sigma,a) tau = 0; current draft rebases on EIF but preserves the same frontier shape
eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1: variance-gap/quotient-norm shape already attempted, though with BJS baseline and unresolved projection object

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship focal objects C(pi,Sigma,G,T), Omega, and P_m^star are promised as computable objects, but Exhibit 9.1 simply assumes primitive contrast rows rather than deriving them from Section 6 incidence matrices Z_m, projections P_m, pi, and Sigma; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship until the object is actually computed from primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The CS and Sun-Abraham comparator gap is cited mostly by bibkey/section range, but no equation, theorem, or page-level anchor identifies the exact missing IF-equivalence frontier being tightened.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The Chen-Sant'Anna-Xie EIF benchmark is used as the load-bearing comparator, but the proposal gives no location-level anchor to the specific EIF or efficiency-bound formula being quotient-refined.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem invokes psi_EIF but only cites Assumptions support-target, omitting Assumption eif, which is the only place the published EIF benchmark is assumed to exist.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Section 6 says A_m maps tau into the excess IF projection psi_m - psi_EIF, but then defines C using A_m - A_EIF with A_EIF := A_BJS; this mixes excess-loadings and absolute EIF loadings.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The variance-gap identity needs an explicit tangent-space orthogonality/Pythagorean condition; as written Assumption eif alone does not justify dropping the cross-covariance term.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The corrected projection formula P_m^star=P_m-P_m C_m^T(C_mP_mC_m^T)^+C_mP_m has undefined compatible spaces: P_m is a nuisance-design projection while C_m acts on treated-cell ATT loadings.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Exhibit 9.1 with a real computation: list Z_BJS, Z_SA, Z_CS, Z_W for T=4, form the Sigma-weighted P_m matrices, derive A_m and C_m, then report C tau and the variance norm.

Add location anchors for the load-bearing comparator formulas: BJS efficiency/imputation theorem, CS group-time ATT/DR IF formula, SA interaction-weighted construction, Wooldridge equivalence result, and Chen-Sant'Anna-Xie EIF display.
Restate the loadings consistently as either absolute IF loadings or excess-over-EIF loadings, then make C the noncircular derived quotient matrix rather than a definition of equivalence.
Add an explicit orthogonality assumption or lemma tying the EIF tangent-space projection to the variance-gap formula, including the exact condition under which the cross term vanishes.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: derive C/Omega/P_m^star from the finite design primitives in a worked T=4 exhibit, fix the loading notation, and add equation-level comparator anchors before resubmitting.
